capabilities: Implement reflection loops in reasoning agents by defining success metrics and validation criteria to ensure reliable and valid outcomes.

procedure.action: Specify success metrics for reflection loops by using domain-specific tools to identify key performance indicators such as error_count and result_validity by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.
Invoke validation checks by asserting that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for each reflection loop iteration, ensuring error-free operations.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail after each reflection loop execution to maintain a record of performance and outcomes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke domain-appropriate tooling to add criteria and metrics for evaluating reflection loop performance, focusing on reliability and validity of results by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Continuously monitor reflection loop operations by setting alerts for any deviations in error_count or result_validity, ensuring prompt detection of issues using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Establish success metrics for implementing reflection loops in reasoning agents using derived knowledge bases, ensuring error-free operations and valid results.
title: Reflection Loop Success Metrics for Reasoning Agents

failureConditions: Inaccurate reasoning when reflection loop metrics are not defined, causing undetected errors and invalid results.
Operational failures when error_count exceeds threshold, leading to unreliable reasoning agent performance.
Loss of audit trail integrity when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, causing difficulty in tracing issues.
